concerns.text: T027 (calculate_compression_ratio.py) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on outputs from T023 (per-trace scores) and T026b (global rules). A task consuming artifacts from two other tasks cannot be parallel-safe with them. The [P] tag is incorrect and violates the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule.

T035 (stats.py) is marked [P] but depends on T035b (calculate_deltas.py) for its input data ('accuracy_deltas.csv'). The task description explicitly states 'This must run before T035' (referring to T035b running before T035), yet T035 is tagged [P]. This creates a hidden ordering requirement that contradicts the parallel tag.

T023 (rule_induction.py) is marked [P] but its description states it must 'Load feature_matrix.csv and split into training... and held-out... sets (matching T012 split)'. While T012 generates the split, T023 relies on the *existence* of the split logic or the specific file paths generated by T012. If T012 is not completed, T023 cannot run. The [P] tag implies independence from T012's completion state, which is false. T023 must strictly follow T012.

T050 updates quickstart.md to document execution order. While the content is correct, the task itself is placed in Phase 7 (Data Integrity) rather than Phase 1 (Setup) or Phase 2 (Foundational). Documentation of the execution order is a foundational prerequisite for the team to understand the dependencies; placing it at the end risks the team executing tasks out of order during the implementation of Phases 3-5.
